data.title: A Look At StandardAero (SARO) Valuation After Recent S&P Index Additions
data.description:
StandardAero (SARO) was recently added to several S&P indices, including the S&P 1000, S&P Composite 1500, S&P 400, and S&P 400 Industrials sector, drawing fresh attention to the stock.

See our latest analysis for StandardAero.

Those index additions have come alongside building momentum in the share price, with a 30 day share price return of 13.07% and a year to date share price return of 11.23%, contributing to a 1 year total shareholder return of 26.61% at a latest share price of $32.97.

If this aerospace move has your attention, it could be a good moment to scan other opportunities among aerospace and defense stocks to see what else is attracting fresh interest.

So with StandardAero trading at $32.97, sitting close to analyst price targets and showing recent share price momentum, is there still mispricing here for patient investors or is the market already baking in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 7.1% Undervalued

StandardAero's most followed narrative pegs fair value around $35.50, slightly above the recent $32.97 close. This puts the current price in focus for valuation driven investors.

Accelerating LEAP engine inductions, a growing backlog and expanding OEM authorized repair content position StandardAero to scale toward roughly $1 billion of LEAP revenue in the next few years. This is expected to support sustained double digit top line growth and higher earnings as the program turns margin positive in 2026.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what earnings profile sits behind that story? The narrative leans heavily on higher margins, a richer mix of work and a premium earnings multiple. The full set of assumptions is where things get interesting.

Result: Fair Value of $35.50 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this story could be knocked off course if the LEAP and CFM56 programs remain at zero margin or if persistent parts shortages continue to weigh on cash conversion.

Find out about the key risks to this StandardAero narrative.

Another View: Rich Multiple, Different Message

That 7.1% undervalued story sits awkwardly next to the current P/E of 59.7x, which is richer than both peers at 40.2x and the wider US Aerospace & Defense group at 41.1x, and also well above a fair ratio of 30.9x. This premium may limit the margin for error.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

data.title: Is Coty’s (COTY) Beauty Pipeline Enough To Outrun Sector Headwinds And Mixed Wall Street Views?
data.description:
• In recent days, several Wall Street firms updated their views on Coty, maintaining mixed ratings while adjusting outlooks amid sector-wide beauty headwinds.
  • At the same time, Coty’s management is leaning heavily on a packed innovation pipeline, spanning new fragrances and premium launches, to reinvigorate organic revenue growth.
  • We’ll now examine how Coty’s innovation-led product pipeline shapes its broader investment narrative following the latest shift in analyst sentiment.

These 9 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is Coty's Investment Narrative?

To own Coty today, you have to believe that its turnaround hinges on two levers: an innovation-heavy product pipeline and a cleaner, more focused operating model under new leadership. The recent flurry of analyst moves, with TD Cowen trimming its target and Barclays staying cautious while Jefferies initiates at Hold, largely reinforces existing short term catalysts rather than redefining them. The story still centers on whether Coty can turn a packed slate of Prestige and ultra premium launches into sustained, positive like for like growth after a tough stretch for beauty. What does change slightly is the balance of risk: a sharp share price rebound over the past month, paired with mixed analyst sentiment and a CEO transition, puts more scrutiny on execution and the timing of any return to profitability.

However, investors should be aware of one execution risk that is easy to underestimate. Coty's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Seven Simply Wall St Community members see Coty’s fair value between US$3.69 and US$9.78, underscoring how far opinions can stretch. Set this against near term execution risks around leadership change and innovation delivery, and you have a stock where your own expectations for consistency in sales recovery truly matter.

data.title: Dividend Aristocrats Inclusion And Record-Seeking 2026 Plan Might Change The Case For Investing In Mullen Group (TSX:MTL)
data.description:
• Mullen Group Ltd. recently declared a monthly dividend of C$0.07 per common share, payable on February 17, 2026 to shareholders of record on January 31, 2026, and outlined 2025–2026 revenue guidance alongside an approved 2026 business plan targeting record performance.
  • The company’s upcoming inclusion in the S&P/TSX Canadian Dividend Aristocrats Index underscores its established dividend track record and reinforces its income-oriented appeal for investors.
  • We’ll now examine how Mullen Group’s Dividend Aristocrats inclusion and record-focused 2026 business plan shape its broader investment narrative.

These 9 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is Mullen Group's Investment Narrative?

For Mullen Group, the big-picture belief is that a steady, acquisition-supported trucking and logistics platform can translate into durable cash generation, even when freight markets are merely “okay” rather than strong. The reaffirmed C$0.07 monthly dividend and upcoming Dividend Aristocrats inclusion fit neatly into that story, reinforcing the income angle and potentially broadening the shareholder base, but they do not radically change the near term catalysts. Those still hinge on execution against the C$2.1 billion 2025 and C$2.3–2.4 billion 2026 revenue targets, integration of recent acquisitions and any surprise in the upcoming Q4 2025 results. On the risk side, elevated debt, thinner profit margins and a freight backdrop that management itself calls challenging remain front and center, even with the “record year” 2026 business plan on the table.

However, one risk in particular could matter more than income-focused investors might expect. Mullen Group's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 18%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community fair value views for Mullen Group range from C$15.00 to about C$20.44, showing wide differences in what investors think the shares are worth. Set those opinions against the company’s record-focused 2026 plan and the reliance on acquisitions in a still-tough freight market, and you can see why some investors may weigh income visibility against execution and balance sheet risks very differently.

data.title: Will Royalty Pharma's (RPRX) Dividend Hike and Guidance Reset Change Its Efficiency and Growth Narrative
data.description:
• Royalty Pharma, which acquires rights to portions of biopharmaceutical sales instead of conducting its own research, recently raised its dividend to US$0.235 payable in March 2026 and issued higher guidance ahead of its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 results scheduled for February 11, 2026.
  • This comes after two years of flat sales and a 4.4 percentage point drop in adjusted operating margin over five years, highlighting the tension between investor optimism and questions about how the company can improve efficiency and growth, especially amid concerns about low trading liquidity.
  • With the dividend increase signaling management’s capital-allocation priorities, we now examine how these developments shape Royalty Pharma’s broader investment narrative.

Outshine the giants: these 23 early-stage AI stocks could fund your retirement.

Advertisement

What Is Royalty Pharma's Investment Narrative?

To own Royalty Pharma, you have to be comfortable with a business that swaps drug development risk for exposure to long-lived royalty streams, and accept that growth depends on disciplined capital deployment rather than blockbuster discoveries. The recent uplift in guidance and the dividend increase to US$0.235 reinforce that management is leaning into a cash-return story even as sales have been broadly flat and margins have slipped over time. In the near term, the key catalyst is whether the February 11 results and outlook translate that optimism into clearer evidence of improving efficiency and a healthier earnings trajectory. At the same time, higher payouts and ongoing buybacks could amplify existing concerns about balance sheet leverage and relatively low trading liquidity, which may become more important if sentiment turns.

However, one risk in particular could catch income-focused investors off guard. Royalty Pharma's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The five fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community stretch from US$39.10 to a very large US$179.83, underlining how far apart individual views can be. Set against recent guidance and margin pressure, that spread invites you to weigh how much execution risk and liquidity constraint you think the current price already reflects.

Explore 5 other fair value estimates on Royalty Pharma - why the stock might be worth just $39.10!

data.title: Is A US$64 Million Pennsylvania Cloud Contract Reframing The Investment Case For ICF International (ICFI)?
data.description:
• ICF International recently announced a US$64 million contract from the Pennsylvania Insurance Department to modernize and operate the state’s environmental claims management system for underground storage tanks over a potential 10-year term.
  • This work highlights how ICF blends cloud migration, data analytics and automation with on-the-ground policy expertise to help governments improve complex indemnification programs.
  • With the company’s shares posting a 7-day gain, we’ll explore how this long-term cloud-based Fund Management System contract shapes ICF’s investment narrative.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

What Is ICF International's Investment Narrative?

To own ICF today, you really have to believe in its ability to turn a solid backlog of government and public-sector work into steadier growth, even after a year of softer revenue and margins. The new US$64 million Pennsylvania Fund Management System contract, alongside the nearly US$300 million EU communications awards, reinforces ICF’s positioning in cloud, data and citizen engagement, but likely does not transform the near-term story on its own. The more immediate catalyst is how Q4 2025 results on February 26 line up with guidance after recent share price strength and a year of underperformance. At the same time, investors still need to watch issues like debt levels, modest return on equity and leadership transition in the finance and operating roles, which this contract win does not resolve.

However, one key financial risk remains that investors should not overlook. ICF International's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 26%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The two fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community, between US$103.25 and US$131.19, show how differently private investors are valuing ICF. data.title: Assessing Boot Barn Holdings (BOOT) Valuation After Strong Quarterly Growth And A More Cautious Outlook
data.description:
Boot Barn Holdings (BOOT) is back in focus after its latest quarterly update, which paired 18.7% revenue growth and high single digit same store sales gains with relatively cautious full year guidance, leaving investors weighing how durable that momentum might be.

See our latest analysis for Boot Barn Holdings.

The recent pullback, with a 7 day share price return of 4.52% and a 90 day share price return of 8.95%, comes after a strong multi year run that includes a 1 year total shareholder return of 9.02% and a 5 year total shareholder return of more than triple the starting level. This suggests shorter term momentum has cooled even as longer term holders remain comfortably ahead.

If Boot Barn's latest results have you thinking about where growth stories might come from next, it could be a good moment to check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

So with revenue up 18.7%, guidance set more cautiously, and the share price about 26% below the average analyst target, is Boot Barn undervalued here, or is the market already pricing in its future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 19% Undervalued

Boot Barn Holdings most followed narrative pegs fair value around $227 per share, compared with the recent $183.98 close, setting up a clear valuation gap for investors to interpret.

Increasing penetration of higher margin exclusive brands (now exceeding 40% of sales, with a target of 50% in coming years) is supporting merchandise margin expansion. This transition to exclusive brands, supported by targeted marketing initiatives and new sourcing strategies, positions the company for improved net margins and long term profitability.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what has to happen for that margin story to hold up? Revenue growth, earnings power, and the future earnings multiple all sit at the core of this fair value case. The full narrative spells out how those pieces fit together without assuming everything goes perfectly.

Result: Fair Value of $227.14 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that upside story could fray if aggressive store growth weighs on returns, or if fashion shifts and online competition cool demand for core western and workwear categories.

Find out about the key risks to this Boot Barn Holdings narrative.

Another View: Higher Multiple, Higher Bar

Analysts see upside to fair value, but the current P/E of 26.9x tells a different story. It sits well above the US Specialty Retail industry at 19.9x, the peer average at 17.2x, and even the 18x fair ratio our work suggests the market could drift toward over time.

If that gap closes, it could mean the earnings story needs to keep impressing just to justify today’s price, rather than stretch for more upside. Which side of that tradeoff do you think is more realistic right now?

data.title: Is Mercury’s New US$60 Million Strategic Weapons And Space Wins Altering The Investment Case For Mercury Systems (MRCY)?
data.description:
• In January 2026, Mercury Systems announced more than US$60.00 million in contracts tied to U.S. strategic weapons and national security space programs, including a development extension on a major radiation-hardened data and signal processing effort through 2031 and new radiation-tolerant storage and processing subsystems using AMD Versal AI Core FPGAs.
  • An interesting aspect of these awards is that Mercury’s radiation-tolerant wideband storage and processing subsystem has now secured a second customer adoption, suggesting deeper integration of its technology into critical space architectures after a successful critical design review.
  • Next, we’ll examine how Mercury’s long-term extension on its strategic weapons work shapes the company’s investment narrative for investors.

Trump's oil boom is here - pipelines are primed to profit. Discover the 22 US stocks riding the wave.

Advertisement

What Is Mercury Systems' Investment Narrative?

To own Mercury Systems, you have to believe its niche in radiation-hardened, high-performance processing for space and strategic weapons can eventually translate into consistent profitability, not just headline program wins. The new US$60.00 million in contracts and the extension of its strategic weapons work through 2031 help underpin revenue visibility and may ease some concern around near term execution risk ahead of the upcoming Q2 2026 earnings release. At the same time, the stock has already run very hard over the past year, it is still loss making, and it trades well above several fair value estimates, so the bar for future performance looks higher. The recent awards fit the bullish narrative, but they do not remove balance sheet leverage, execution risk on complex programs, or valuation risk if expectations reset.

Mercury Systems' share price has been on the slide but might be dropping deeper into value territory. Find out whether it's a bargain at this price.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates cluster between about US$52.52 and US$86.00, underlining how differently investors are framing Mercury’s story. Set that against a business that remains unprofitable and heavily reliant on converting contract momentum into sustainable margins, and the case for comparing multiple viewpoints before taking a stance becomes clear.

data.title: Does AIT’s Q1 Earnings Beat and Industrial Uptick Change The Bull Case For Applied Industrial Technologies (AIT)?
data.description:
• Applied Industrial Technologies recently posted first-quarter fiscal 2026 results that exceeded earnings and revenue expectations, supported by strength across core industrial distribution and engineered solutions operations.
  • This outperformance, reinforced by favorable analyst earnings estimate revisions and commentary about improving industrial sector conditions, has reinforced investor attention on the company’s execution and market positioning.

What Is Applied Industrial Technologies' Investment Narrative?

To own Applied Industrial Technologies, you have to believe in a steady, execution-led story rather than a blockbuster growth one. The recent earnings beat and new high in the share price validate the company’s focus on high-quality industrial distribution and engineered solutions, but they also arguably pull some of the easy short term upside forward. Near term catalysts now center on whether upcoming quarters can sustain the raised fiscal 2026 guidance and keep earnings revisions moving in the right direction, especially if industrial sector recovery proves uneven. At the same time, the stock’s richer valuation multiples and modest forecast growth leave less room for disappointment if orders soften or margins come under pressure. In that sense, the latest results improve the narrative, but also raise the execution bar.

data.title: Should Rapiblyk’s Expanded Distribution Through Cardinal Health Signal a Deeper Shift in CAH’s Specialty Strategy?
data.description:

What Is Cardinal Health's Investment Narrative?

For Cardinal Health, the big-picture belief is that its massive distribution footprint and specialty focus can keep translating into steady, if unspectacular, growth and cash returns, even with a relatively full valuation. The Rapiblyk distribution news fits that story neatly: it reinforces Cardinal Health’s relevance in critical care channels, but on its own it is unlikely to move the needle against the company’s US$234,310.0 million revenue base. Near term, the more important catalysts still look like execution in specialty therapeutics, efficiency gains from automation, and disciplined capital allocation through buybacks and dividends. On the risk side, investors have to stay comfortable with high leverage, thin profit margins and a price to earnings multiple above both industry and peer averages. The Rapiblyk deal adds incremental support to the momentum narrative rather than redefining it.

data.description:

Why Glaukos Is Back on Investors’ Radar

Glaukos (GKOS) has attracted fresh attention after issuing preliminary fourth quarter and full year 2025 revenue figures, reaffirming its 2026 sales outlook, and securing FDA approval for its Epioxa corneal health product.

See our latest analysis for Glaukos.

After a sharp 58.5% 90 day share price return and a 3.2% 30 day share price gain, Glaukos has pulled back 4.7% over the last day. Its 1 year total shareholder return of a 25.1% loss contrasts with a 3 year total shareholder return of a 149.8% gain, suggesting that recent enthusiasm around iDose TR, Epioxa and the updated guidance is now being weighed against execution risks and past volatility.

If Glaukos has caught your eye, it could be a good moment to see what else is happening across ophthalmology and beyond by scanning healthcare stocks for other potential ideas.

With the share price up sharply over 90 days, a value score of 2, a reported intrinsic discount of 56.7% and a net loss of US$87.6 million, you have to ask: is this a buying opportunity, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 5.8% Undervalued

Glaukos closed at $119.98, compared with a widely followed fair value narrative of about $127.31, which leans on strong growth and margin assumptions.

Strong ongoing adoption and utilization of iDose TR, a first of its kind procedural pharmaceutical with a unique profile, suggests the early stages of a paradigm shift toward interventional glaucoma therapies, a substantial long term opportunity given the aging population and rising prevalence of glaucoma, likely driving robust multi year revenue and market expansion.

data.title: A Look At Butterfly Network (BFLY) Valuation After Upbeat Guidance And Renewed Analyst Optimism
data.description:
Advertisement

Why the latest guidance and analyst sentiment matter

Butterfly Network (BFLY) has drawn fresh attention after guiding for at least 17% year over year revenue growth in the fourth quarter of 2025, with several brokerage firms reiterating positive views on the stock.

For you as an investor, that combination of explicit growth guidance and supportive analyst commentary creates a clear focal point: how much confidence you place in Butterfly’s ability to translate handheld ultrasound adoption into sustained revenue and a path toward profitability.

See our latest analysis for Butterfly Network.

Those guidance comments and bullish analyst updates have come after a sharp swing in sentiment. Butterfly’s share price is at US$4.51 with a 90 day share price return of 105%, which contrasts with a 5 year total shareholder return of a 75.56% loss. This suggests momentum has picked up recently while the long term picture remains mixed.

If Butterfly’s move has you rethinking where growth in medical technology could come from next, it might be worth scanning the wider market through healthcare stocks.

With BFLY at US$4.51, fresh guidance for at least 17% Q4 2025 revenue growth and analyst targets only modestly higher, investors may need to consider whether there is still a buying opportunity or if the market is already pricing in that future growth.

Ongoing advances in semiconductor manufacturing, new product launches (e.g., iQ Station, upcoming fourth-generation P5 technology), and cost containment efforts are driving product miniaturization and lower production costs, supporting higher gross margins and a credible path to sustainable profitability at scale as portable imaging adoption accelerates globally.

data.title: Commerce Bancshares (CBSH) Valuation After Record Q4 Results And FineMark Acquisition Completion
data.description:
Commerce Bancshares (CBSH) stock action is being shaped by fresh fourth quarter results, including record revenues, solid earnings and share repurchases, as well as the completed FineMark acquisition that expands its wealth management and private banking reach.

See our latest analysis for Commerce Bancshares.

Those record fourth quarter numbers and the FineMark deal come after a mixed stretch for investors, with a 90 day share price return of 5.26% and a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 13.78%, suggesting short term momentum has picked up while longer term returns have been much softer.

If this kind of bank earnings story has your attention, it could be a good moment to widen your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With shares down on a 1 year view but trading at a reported 47% discount to one intrinsic value estimate and below the average analyst target, you have to ask: is there a genuine opportunity here, or is the market already factoring in future growth?

Advertisement

Preferred P/E of 13.8x: Is it justified?

Commerce Bancshares currently trades on a P/E of 13.8x, which prices the stock above the wider US banks industry average of 11.8x but roughly in line with the 14x peer group average.

The P/E ratio links the current share price to earnings. A higher multiple usually means the market is asking you to pay more today for each dollar of profit. For a bank like CBSH, that often reflects views on the quality and durability of earnings rather than rapid growth expectations.

Here, the picture is mixed. On one hand, earnings have grown 3.6% per year over the past 5 years, with a stronger 8.6% earnings growth figure over the last year and current net profit margins of 33.1% compared to 32% a year ago. On the other hand, CBSH's Return on Equity is 15%, which is classed as low against a 20% benchmark. In addition, earnings are forecast to grow 6.7% per year, slower than both the broader US market and the US banks industry.

Compared with the US banks industry average P/E of 11.8x and an estimated fair P/E of 12.4x, CBSH's 13.8x multiple sits at a premium level. The market could potentially move toward aligning with that fair ratio over time. This suggests investors are currently paying more than both the industry and the modelled fair level for each dollar of earnings, despite growth forecasts that are not especially high.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Commerce Bancshares

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 13.8x (OVERVALUED)

However, the recent 1 year total shareholder return decline of 13.78% and a value score of 3 hint that sentiment and valuation risks could easily resurface.

Find out about the key risks to this Commerce Bancshares narrative.

Another view: DCF says the stock looks cheap

While the 13.8x P/E suggests Commerce Bancshares trades at a premium, our DCF model presents a different picture. With the shares at $53.21 versus an estimated future cash flow value of $100.24, the model indicates a significant discount and a potential mismatch in expectations.

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.
